description: The adjective naming limited spatial extension, the quality of not reaching far from beginning to end. In Phi, shortness is observed without negative judgment—short things can be complete, efficient, appropriate. This word names the quality of contained extent in space or, metaphorically, in time.
sound_symbolism: The word opens with 'te', where the dental stop 't' creates immediate boundary—the sound of stopping, of limit reached quickly. The clear 'e' focuses on the extent. This closes into 'ku', where the velar stop 'k' reinforces ending and the deep 'u' compacts depth into small space. Two syllables appropriately contain the word.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective preceding nouns: 'teku ruela' (short path). Can describe physical objects, durations, or distances. Distinguished from 'laeno' (long) as dimensional opposite, without negative connotation.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Short supply chains, short commutes, short feedback loops often serve sustainability. 'Teku' supports valuing appropriate brevity.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Short can be complete—a short meditation can go deep, a short life can be well-lived. 'Teku' names brevity without judgment.
